subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and consistent use of headings guides the reader through introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion
Logical progression from hypothesis through testing to conclusion is easy to follow
Concise focus on investigation-relevant material with minimal irrelevant content
weaknesses: Occasional minor logical jumps (e.g., quoting results before full explanation) interrupt flow
Some unnecessary repetition of standard definitions
Formatting issues (overflowing tables, non-sequential equation numbering) detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and well organized, with a clear introduction, body and conclusion, and most content is directly relevant to the investigation. Minor jumps in narrative and some formatting inconsistencies prevent the work from meeting the highest band, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Consistent use of standard symbols (Î¼, Ï, ln(x), z) with numbered equations for easy reference
Effective integration of multiple representations (raw-data tables, histograms, Q-Q plots, formulae) to support analysis
Good logical structure in explanations, with clear transitions between calculation steps and interpretation
weaknesses: Minor notation inconsistencies (upper/lower-case Z, subscript confusion) interrupt clarity
Some images lack axis labels or parameter values, reducing interpretability
Occasional stray LaTeX artifacts and incomplete reproducibility details (software/method)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical notation and language are used consistently and accurately, multiple representations are effectively employed, and communication is clear overall. Small notational and labeling lapses prevent a top score, so band 3 is justified.

strengths: Original data collection from 86 peers shows genuine initiative and independent thinking
The experiment design (time limits, no calculators) reflects a personal approach rooted in the studentâs context
Selection of multiple complementary normality tests demonstrates initiative beyond a single method
weaknesses: Investigation remains within conventional statistical procedures without novel modelling or derivations
Proposed extensions are not fully developed with anticipated challenges or detailed planning
Personal insights, while clear, do not constitute highly innovative or unique approaches
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but it does not reach the level of innovation or exceptional creativity needed for the highest band, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on results throughout, linking histogram skewness to Q-Q plot behaviour
Thoughtful analysis of each normality testâs outcome and discussion of methodological limitations
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future work (alternative distributions, deeper error analysis)
weaknesses: Depth of quantitative assessment of error sources could be expanded
Critical insights are present but could be woven more continuously into the narrative
Some reflections stop short of exploring broader theoretical implications in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is substantial and critical, with meaningful analysis of results and clear evaluation of implications. Although further quantitative depth could strengthen the work, the consistent critical reflection meets the descriptor for band 3.

strengths: Appropriate use of logarithmic transformation, standardisation and geometric mean calculations directly aligned to the aim
Correct application of four different normality assessments with sound interpretation of results
Demonstrates thorough understanding of concepts (e.g., link between skewness and Q-Q plot deviations)
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies and lack of deeper derivations of test statistics
No extension into higher-level HL mathematics or confidence-interval estimation
Some suggestions for strengthening HL rigor (formal Weberâs law expression) remain undeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is relevant, accurate and commensurate with the SL level, demonstrating solid understanding and application. Absence of HL-level depth and minor errors limit the score to band 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each calculation step and test, and transitions between sections are explicit, giving good logical structure.  Occasional typographical noise (e.g., stray LaTeX artefacts) detracts from absolute clarity, but the argument remains understandable without external guidance.  This fits the descriptor âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ, i.e., band 3.

Calculations of Î¼, Ï, z-scores, percentage ranks, empirical probabilities and Anderson-Darling critical values are mostly accurate, with only minor rounding inconsistencies.  Conceptual understanding is sound, as shown when the student links right-tail deviations in the Q-Q plot to positive skewness.  Minor slips and lack of deeper derivation prevent a band 4 or above, so band 3 is awarded.

The student employs raw-data tables, standardised-value tables, histograms, Q-Q plots, and formulae, each serving a distinct purpose in the argument.  These representations are appropriate and generally well integrated, satisfying âeffective useâ.  A richer variety (e.g., box-and-whisker plots or cumulative frequency graphs) and clearer labelling on some images would be needed for the âhighly effectiveâ top band, so band 3 is selected.

The exploration moves methodically from inspiration, hypothesis and data collection through to analysis and conclusion, so the reader can follow the argument without difficulty.  Headings are used consistently and each section connects sensibly to the next (e.g., the logarithmic-perception rationale leads naturally into testing for lognormality).  A few minor jumps occur â such as quoting the geometric mean result before stating the true pasta count â but overall the logical flow remains clear and coherent rather than merely âsomeâ.  Hence band 3 is appropriate, while the polish expected for band 4 (seamless, sophisticated development) is not fully reached.

Selecting and executing multiple statistical tests, calculating critical values manually and proposing extensions such as fitting Poisson or exponential models display a good level of initiative.  However, the investigation remains within conventional statistical territory, so the creativity does not reach the exceptional level required for band 3.

The student analyses why pasta passes but grains fail the lognormal model, links histogram skewness to Q-Q plot behaviour, and recognises the influence of ties on the Anderson-Darling test.  This constitutes thoughtful, although not âdeep and insightfulâ, analysis, so band 2 is appropriate.

Outcomes are evaluated via comparison to true counts and by acknowledging sampling bias and methodological limitations.  Future implications (e.g., exploring other distributions) are suggested, meeting the âclear evaluationâ descriptor.  Greater quantitative assessment of error sources would be needed for comprehensive band 3.

Reflection appears at several stages: interpretation of each normality test, discussion of the geometric meanâs suitability, and a dedicated strengths/weaknesses section.  The commentary moves beyond description into some insight, yet stops short of deep critical reflection woven throughout the work.  This aligns with âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ (band 2).

Symbols such as Î¼, Ï, ln(x) and z are introduced and used consistently, and equation numbering helps reference them.  Minor slips occur (mixing upper- and lower-case Z, occasional subscript confusion in Table 2), preventing a fully âconsistent and accurateâ score, but overall usage is mostly correct, warranting band 3.

The study is clearly rooted in the studentâs curiosity about the âwisdom of the crowdâ and their school context; the jars, time limit and prohibition on calculators create a personal experimental design.  While effective, the approach is not highly innovative (e.g., no unique modelling framework or novel metric), placing it in the âclear evidenceâ rather than âhighly personal and innovativeâ category.

Nearly all material directly supports the investigation; long raw-data tables and multi-paragraph quotations are necessary for transparency rather than filler.  Occasional repetition (re-explaining the standard normal after already defining it) and some superfluous citations of widely known facts mean the piece is not âhighly conciseâ, but it is more than âmostly relevantâ.  Band 3 therefore best matches the balance of conciseness and relevance.

Logarithmic transformation, standardisation, geometric mean calculation and four different normality assessments are all relevant to the stated aim and are applied correctly.  However, the mathematics does not extend to higher-level HL content such as derivations of test statistics or confidence-interval estimation, so ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3) is the best fit.

Collecting original data from 86 peers, choosing which distribution to test, and selecting four complementary normality checks demonstrate significant personal decision-making beyond textbook procedure.  Nonetheless, the mathematics largely follows standard tests rather than novel derivations, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.  Band 2 is therefore justified.

There is a clear introduction, body and conclusion, with tables, graphs and formulae placed close to the text that references them.  Numbered subsections and captions aid navigation, showing good organisation.  Some tables (e.g. Table 2) run over formatting boundaries and a few figures are awkwardly inserted, so the document falls short of the âexceptionally well organisedâ description required for band 4, but it is comfortably âwell organised with clear structureâ, meriting band 3.

The techniques (lognormal/normal linkage, Anderson-Darling test, Q-Q plots) are squarely within the HL syllabus and are neither below nor beyond it.  They are used correctly but without the depth or complexity that would demonstrate âfully commensurateâ or âexpertâ application.  Thus band 3 is selected.

annotations.comment: The lognormal PDF is correctly stated and numbered; adding âfor x>0â would clarify the domain explicitly.
The description of the Q-Q plot logic is clear; note which software or method was used to generate these plots for reproducibility.
The title is clear and sets out the investigationâs focus effectively, aiding reader orientation from the outset.
The historical example is well explained but could be more concise; consider trimming some background to sharpen focus on your own study.
Consider ending the introduction with an explicit statement of your aim to improve logical flow and guide the reader smoothly into the methodology.
The connection to Weberâs law is insightful; including the formal law expression $$\Delta I/I = k$$ would strengthen the mathematical basis.
Figure 1 illustrates perception well; adding axis labels or a brief note on dot counts would improve clarity.
The rationale for choosing lognormal is reasonable; briefly discuss why alternative distributions (e.g., gamma) were less appropriate to deepen the argument.
The aim is precisely stated and focuses the investigation clearly, aiding reader understanding of scope and purpose.
Image captions are clear, but including a scale or approximate jar dimensions would strengthen interpretation of the context.
The raw data table is comprehensive but would be easier to read if split into separate tables for pasta and grains rather than repeating columns.
Data collection protocol is described well; specify whether jar positions were randomized to eliminate order effects.
Insightful note on rounding bias; link this observation to its impact on the AndersonâDarling test outcomes for stronger critical analysis.
Figure 2 effectively contrasts distributions; including explicit parameter values (Î¼,Ï) in the caption would improve mathematical communication.
Standardization formula is correct; clarify explicitly that Î¼ and Ï refer to log-transformed data to avoid confusion.
Table 2 contains valuable standardized data but inconsistent header notation (Z_q vs z_p) interrupts clarity; ensure uniform symbols.
The histogram is well integrated, but adding numerical axis labels and binâwidth information would enhance interpretability.
Sideâbyâside presentation of pasta and grains histograms would allow direct visual comparison of symmetry and skewness.
Equation numbering jumps here; maintain sequential numbering to support easy referencing (e.g., (4) then (5)).
Comparison table is helpful; adding a theoretical column side by side with observed values would improve clarity of deviations.
Percentile rank formula is correct; mention alternative conventions (e.g., (rank)/(n+1)) and justify your choice briefly.
Interpretation links findings to geometric mean effectively; explore further why perceptual processes yield a lognormal distribution more deeply.
Graph 4 quantifies fit with RÂ²; discuss what RÂ² threshold you consider acceptable to justify normality assumption.
The AndersonâDarling formula is precisely stated; a brief note on why terms pair i and n+1âi would improve reader comprehension.
Including the critical value alongside AÂ² results in Table 4 would allow immediate visual comparison of test statistic and threshold.
Proposed extensions show initiative; outlining a brief plan or anticipated challenges (e.g., parameter estimation) would strengthen personal engagement.
